title: Some queries
body:
@cliffordwolf 

I had some questions related to how Yosys works; I asked them in the webchat but no one responds.

Please help me.

1.  For any source code, do we get AST2 from AST1? I think the answer is yes.

2. For any source code, if I want to make changes in the AST slightly and recreate the netlist using write_verilog (which as I understand, converts the AST to platform-specific Verilog netlists), which module of the Yosys do I have to make the changes in?

**Example: Suppose for this source code:** 

module top
  (
   input CLK,
   input RST,
   input enable,
   input [31:0] value,
   output [7:0] led
  );
  reg [31:0] count;
  reg [7:0] state;
  assign led = count[23:16];
  always @(posedge CLK) begin
    if(RST) begin                            
      count <= 0;
      state <= 0;
    end else begin
      if(state == 0) begin
        if(enable) state <= 1;
      end else if(state == 1) begin
        state <= 2;
      end else if(state == 2) begin
        count <= count + value;
        state <= 0;
      end
    end
  end
endmodule

**I get this AST1 :** 

    AST_MODULE <new.v:1> [0x17a16a0] str='\top'
      AST_WIRE <new.v:3> [0x179d640] str='\CLK' input port=1
      AST_WIRE <new.v:4> [0x179d740] str='\RST' input port=2
      AST_WIRE <new.v:5> [0x179d890] str='\enable' input port=3
      AST_WIRE <new.v:6> [0x179d9c0] str='\value' input port=4
        AST_RANGE <new.v:6> [0x179dd70]
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:6> [0x179db10] bits='00000000000000000000000000011111'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=31
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:6> [0x179dc40] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
      AST_WIRE <new.v:7> [0x179de70] str='\led' output port=5
        AST_RANGE <new.v:7> [0x179e210]
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:7> [0x179dfb0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000111'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=7
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:7> [0x179e0e0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
      AST_WIRE <new.v:9> [0x179e780] str='\count' reg
        AST_RANGE <new.v:9> [0x179e880]
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:9> [0x179e980] bits='00000000000000000000000000011111'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=31
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:9> [0x179ea80] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
      AST_WIRE <new.v:10> [0x179ecf0] str='\state' reg
        AST_RANGE <new.v:10> [0x179edf0]
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:10> [0x179eef0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000111'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=7
          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:10> [0x179eff0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
      AST_ASSIGN <new.v:11> [0x179f240]
        AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:11> [0x179e310] str='\led'
        AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:11> [0x179f140] str='\count'
          AST_RANGE <new.v:11> [0x179ebf0]
            AST_CONSTANT <new.v:11> [0x179e680] bits='00000000000000000000000000010111'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=23
            AST_CONSTANT <new.v:11> [0x179e450] bits='00000000000000000000000000010000'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=16
      AST_ALWAYS <new.v:12> [0x179f340]
        AST_POSEDGE <new.v:12> [0x179f5d0]
          AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:12> [0x179f4d0] str='\CLK'
        AST_BLOCK <new.v:12> [0x179f6d0]
          AST_BLOCK <new.v:13> [0x179f7f0]
            AST_CASE <new.v:13> [0x179f9f0]
              AST_REDUCE_BOOL <new.v:13> [0x179fe50]
                AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:13> [0x179f8f0] str='\RST'
              AST_COND <new.v:13> [0x179fbf0]
                AST_CONSTANT <new.v:13> [0x179fcf0] bits='1'(1) range=[0:0] int=1
                AST_BLOCK <new.v:13> [0x179faf0]
                  AST_BLOCK <new.v:14> [0x179ffb0]
                    AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:14> [0x17a02f0]
                      AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:14> [0x17a00b0] str='\count'
                      AST_CONSTANT <new.v:14> [0x17a01f0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
                    AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:15> [0x17a0660]
                      AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:15> [0x17a03f0] str='\state'
                      AST_CONSTANT <new.v:15> [0x17a0530] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
              AST_COND <new.v:16> [0x17a0860]
                AST_DEFAULT <new.v:16> [0x17a0960]
                AST_BLOCK <new.v:16> [0x17a0760]
                  AST_BLOCK <new.v:17> [0x17a0a80]
                    AST_CASE <new.v:17> [0x17a0f40]
                      AST_REDUCE_BOOL <new.v:17> [0x17a1380]
                        AST_EQ <new.v:17> [0x17a0e40]
                          AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:17> [0x17a0b80] str='\state'
                          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:17> [0x17a0d10] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]
                      AST_COND <new.v:17> [0x17a1140]
                        AST_CONSTANT <new.v:17> [0x17a1240] bits='1'(1) range=[0:0] int=1
                        AST_BLOCK <new.v:17> [0x17a1040]
                          AST_BLOCK <new.v:18> [0x17a5ec0]
                            AST_CASE <new.v:18> [0x17a60c0]
                              AST_REDUCE_BOOL <new.v:18> [0x17a64c0]
                                AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:18> [0x17a5fc0] str='\enable'
                              AST_COND <new.v:18> [0x17a62c0]
                                AST_CONSTANT <new.v:18> [0x17a63c0] bits='1'(1) range=[0:0] int=1
                                AST_BLOCK <new.v:18> [0x17a61c0]
                                  AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:18> [0x17a67f0]
                                    AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:18> [0x17a65c0] str='\state'
                                    AST_CONSTANT <new.v:18> [0x17a66c0] bits='00000000000000000000000000000001'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=1
                      AST_COND <new.v:19> [0x17a69f0]
                        AST_DEFAULT <new.v:19> [0x17a6af0]
                        AST_BLOCK <new.v:19> [0x17a68f0]
                          AST_CASE <new.v:19> [0x17a6f90]
                            AST_REDUCE_BOOL <new.v:19> [0x17a73d0]
                              AST_EQ <new.v:19> [0x17a6e90]
                                AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:19> [0x17a6c30] str='\state'
                                AST_CONSTANT <new.v:19> [0x17a6d60] bits='00000000000000000000000000000001'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=1
                            AST_COND <new.v:19> [0x17a7190]
                              AST_CONSTANT <new.v:19> [0x17a7290] bits='1'(1) range=[0:0] int=1
                              AST_BLOCK <new.v:19> [0x17a7090]
                                AST_BLOCK <new.v:20> [0x17a7530]
                                  AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:20> [0x17a78c0]
                                    AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:20> [0x17a7630] str='\state'
                                    AST_CONSTANT <new.v:20> [0x17a7790] bits='00000000000000000000000000000010'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=2
                            AST_COND <new.v:21> [0x17a7ac0]
                              AST_DEFAULT <new.v:21> [0x17a7bc0]
                              AST_BLOCK <new.v:21> [0x17a79c0]
                                AST_CASE <new.v:21> [0x17a8060]
                                  AST_REDUCE_BOOL <new.v:21> [0x17a8480]
                                    AST_EQ <new.v:21> [0x17a7f60]
                                      AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:21> [0x17a7ce0] str='\state'
                                      AST_CONSTANT <new.v:21> [0x17a7e30] bits='00000000000000000000000000000010'(32) signed range=[31:0] int=2
                                  AST_COND <new.v:21> [0x17a8260]
                                    AST_CONSTANT <new.v:21> [0x17a8360] bits='1'(1) range=[0:0] int=1
                                    AST_BLOCK <new.v:21> [0x17a8160]
                                      AST_BLOCK <new.v:22> [0x17a85e0]
                                        AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:22> [0x17a8b20]
                                          AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:22> [0x17a86e0] str='\count'
                                          AST_ADD <new.v:22> [0x17a89e0]
                                            AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:22> [0x17a87e0] str='\count'
                                            AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:22> [0x17a88e0] str='\value'
                                        AST_ASSIGN_LE <new.v:23> [0x17a8eb0]
                                          AST_IDENTIFIER <new.v:23> [0x17a8c40] str='\state'
                                          AST_CONSTANT <new.v:23> [0x17a8d80] bits='00000000000000000000000000000000'(32) signed range=[31:0]



Now suppose I want to make changes in the AST- like changing the range of str 'led' to some other values like from [7:0] to [8:0]. And then, may be adding some lines in the AST to add some more logic.

Then using that modified AST, I would get new RTLIL and then using write_verilog, new netlists.

Which modules do I need to change in Yosys for that and of course keeping in mind that the modified AST need to be in a format that produces RTLIL which **write_verilog** can use ? Please help.
